chunk_summary: This section outlines a standardized outpatient dietary assessment for macronutrient decisions in childhood, focusing on growth faltering or obesity through reproducible diet history, portion estimation, and actionable dietary swaps.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of a standardized outpatient dietary assessment for children.
Learn how to conduct a two-day dietary recall and beverage/snack audits.
Identify energy displacement patterns and their impact on macronutrient intake.
Apply portion estimation using Indian household measures for actionable dietary advice.
key_facts: A reproducible diet history bridges growth charts and macronutrient prescriptions.
Assessment includes a two-day recall (weekday + weekend/holiday) and audits of beverages, snacks, and screen-time eating.
Portion estimation uses Indian household measures: one katori (dal/curd/khichdi), roti size by palm diameter, one ladle (sabzi), and one cup (200–250 ml for milk/chaas).
Energy displacement often occurs due to excess milk, sweetened beverages, and frequent biscuits, reducing protein- and micronutrient-dense meals.
If intake is adequate but growth falters, consider malabsorption or increased needs and proceed to medical evaluation.
reference_quote: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children (NICE NG75) — 'If intake appears adequate but growth falters, consider increased needs or malabsorption and proceed to medical evaluation rather than endless diet changes.'
document_key_topics: Actionable dietary swaps for pediatric nutrition
